released_episode_demo.limitations: The demo is purposively selected and supplies compact prompts, responses, state text, scores, and a committed flag, but not complete runs, return codes, retry/invalid records, judge rationales, state file paths, annotation rows, or derivation provenance. It cannot reproduce paper tables or denominators.

paper_release_conformance_findings.finding: The released OpenClaw runner does not isolate persist and query phases. Both call oc_turn with the default session_key='pasb'; there is no session reset or new query key between lines 269-278. The paper says conversation history and runtime state are cleared and that carry-over must pass through durable state. Released OpenClaw outcomes therefore do not demonstrate that isolation property.

The released runners do not implement the paper's tested-content commit classifier. Runner records contain captured state but no commit label. audit_run.py instead treats USER content longer than 600 characters, any MEMORY content, or any skill as a commit, without baseline delta or tested-claim matching. Thus the released code cannot reconstruct the paper's claim-specific Commit% or surface matrix.

The paper/dataset card says audit logs, a 50-task human-gold subset, full confusion matrices, and baseline scripts are released. The pinned 32-path repository contains task JSONL and four runner/judge/audit scripts, but no construction audit logs, human-gold labels/confusion matrices, persist-state annotation rows, complete raw/judged runs, or plotting/analysis lineage.

The 336-record website demo is not task-version conformant: 74 OpenClaw episode records across 58 task IDs use persist prompts different from the released canonical task JSONL, while all query prompts match. The release provides no version or transformation ledger for these prompt differences.

Paper Appendix B specifies a 20-second OpenClaw flush and 15-second Hermes flush. Both released runners default PASB_SLEEP_AFTER_PERSIST to 15 seconds. Neither verifies quiescence or records a flush-completion event, so capture timing may miss late writes.

Execution validity is not an unconditional denominator. Hermes retries turns up to ten times but does not retain attempt histories; final timeout/API-failure records can still be written. OpenClaw has no retries. Worker exceptions can omit rows. audit_run.py does not require 1,600 unique task IDs, five successful persist turns, three successful query turns, zero nonzero return codes, or complete judges.

The paper's cross-turn judge template states a 6,000-character state view; released judge.py truncates it to 4,000 characters. Complete paper-time prompt/configuration identity is therefore not established.

bounded_released_lineage.interpretation: This compact record makes one task-to-persist-response-to-captured-text-to-query-response-to-score witness inspectable. It does not preserve file paths, tool/write attempts, baseline diff, flush event, retrieval candidates, model-visible injected state, judge rationale, or retry/invalid lineage, so it cannot prove write causality, retrieval, adoption, or exact table reconstruction.

execution_scope: Static audit only. No paid model calls or untrusted agent frameworks were executed. Released Python files compiled successfully.
